full_prompt: Design a cold-rolled steel sheet-metal L-bracket that accepts a canonical self-clinching nut insert (steel/stainless, #6-32 UNC) (thread #6-32 UNC, for host sheet thickness 1.6 mm / 0.063 in minimum) and transmits its published rated loads without tearing or permanently deforming the host sheet. The representative catalog 2024 values for the #6-32 UNC self-clinching insert installed in low-carbon steel host sheet (hardness HRB 80 maximum per install spec) are: minimum installation force approximately 13.3 kN (3,000 lbf), minimum pull-out force 2.45 kN (550 lbf), minimum torque-out 2.7 N*m (24 in*lbf). The bracket geometry: two-leg sheet metal L formed from 1.6 mm cold-rolled steel (SPCC / DC01, yield 210 MPa, UTS 350 MPa), bend radius 2.4 mm inside, leg A 80 mm long x 50 mm wide with two mounting slots (6 x 12 mm) on a 60 mm centerline spacing, leg B 60 mm long x 50 mm wide carrying the #6-32 self-clinching insert at its centroid (at least 6.5 mm from any edge per minimum edge-distance spec for #6-32 thread). The insert is installed by pressing into the pre-punched 4.19 mm (+0.08/-0.0) host hole, which plastically flows the host sheet into the insert's knurl and undercut. Service loads applied through a #6-32 grade 5 screw threaded into the insert: LC1 axial pull-out load 2.45 kN (full rated) normal to leg B; LC2 installation torque reaction 2.7 N*m on the insert-to-sheet interface; LC3 combined shear 500 N along leg B plus 1 kN normal to leg B. Pass/fail: (R1) at LC1 rated pull-out, the maximum principal stress in the host sheet around the insert must remain below UTS / 1.25 = 280 MPa, i.e. no net-section tearing (self-clinching-insert design-guide SF 1.25 against sheet tear-out at rated pull-out); (R2) at LC2 rated torque-out, the host-sheet von Mises around the insert must stay below yield / 1.0 = 210 MPa on the first torque cycle (insert clinch friction governs, catalog practice); (R3) L-bracket max von Mises under LC3 <= yield / 1.5 = 140 MPa (general sheet-metal SF); (R4) L-bracket deflection at the insert location under LC3 <= 0.5 mm (to preserve screw preload integrity); (R5) host-sheet minimum edge-distance 6.5 mm to any free edge and 9.5 mm between adjacent self-clinching insert hole centerlines (catalog constraint); (R6) host hole diameter tolerance 4.19 +0.08/-0.00 mm (catalog install spec, directly affects retention). The bracket is manufactured by laser-cutting a flat blank, punching the 4.19 mm insert hole, V-die forming to the L shape, and pressing the insert on an arbor press. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the formed L-bracket plus flat-pattern DXF; report listing sheet max principal stress at LC1, von Mises at LC2, bracket max von Mises + deflection at LC3, and a pass/fail line for edge-distance and hole-diameter compliance.
short_prompt:
Design a cold-rolled steel sheet-metal L-bracket that accepts a #6-32 UNC self-clinching nut insert and transmits its rated loads without tearing or permanently deforming the host sheet. Catalog values for #6-32 UNC self-clinching insert in low-carbon steel: minimum installation force ~13.3 kN (3,000 lbf), minimum pull-out 2.45 kN (550 lbf), minimum torque-out 2.7 N*m (24 in*lbf).

Geometry: two-leg L formed from 1.6 mm cold-rolled steel (SPCC / DC01), inside bend radius 2.4 mm. Leg A 80 mm x 50 mm with two 6 x 12 mm mounting slots on 60 mm spacing; Leg B 60 mm x 50 mm carries the insert at its centroid. Host hole 4.19 +0.08/-0.00 mm.

Load cases through a #6-32 grade 5 screw.
- LC1 axial pull-out 2.45 kN normal to leg B.
- LC2 installation torque reaction 2.7 N*m on the insert-to-sheet interface.
- LC3 combined shear: 500 N along leg B plus 1 kN normal to leg B.

Geometric compliance: minimum edge distance 6.5 mm and >= 9.5 mm between adjacent insert centerlines; L-bracket deflection at the insert under LC3 <= 0.5 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Bend allowance and K-factor computation for the formed L is deterministic CAD/CAM, not FEA.
Self-clinching insert install force curves are tabulated, not simulated.

prompt.title: Sheet-metal L-bracket with self-clinching nut insert
prompt.brief: Design a 1.6 mm cold-rolled steel L-bracket with one #6-32 self-clinching nut insert, sized to transmit the catalog 2.45 kN pull-out and 2.7 N*m torque-out without host-sheet tear-out.

boundary_conditions.insert_interface: clinch contact with radial interference, friction + form locking

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: UTS / 1.25 = 350 / 1.25 (self-clinching-insert design-guide tear-out SF at rated pull-out)

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 or HEX20 with elasto-plastic host sheet
analysis_types: nonlinear static with contact + friction at clinch

notes.numeric_source: Representative #6-32 self-clinching insert technical data: min pull-out 2.45 kN (550 lbf), min torque-out 2.7 Nm (24 in-lbf), install force 13.3 kN (3,000 lbf), host sheet min 1.6 mm / max HRB 80.

eval_coverage: formed L-bracket with mixed slotted + insert features
